positive_passage: Machine learning is a subset of artificial intelligence that enables computer systems to learn and improve from experience without being explicitly programmed. It focuses on developing algorithms that can access data and use it to learn for themselves.

A neural network is a series of algorithms that attempts to recognize underlying relationships in a set of data through a process that mimics the way the human brain operates. Neural networks consist of layers of interconnected nodes or neurons that process information.

Deep learning is a subfield of machine learning that uses multi-layered neural networks to learn representations of data with multiple levels of abstraction. It is inspired by the structure and function of the brain and has revolutionized fields like computer vision and NLP.
Natural language processing (NLP) is a branch of artificial intelligence that deals with the interaction between computers and humans using natural language. It enables machines to read
column4: and derive meaning from human language.

positive_passage: A transformer is a deep learning architecture that relies on the self-attention mechanism to process sequential data. Introduced in the paper Attention Is All You Need
column3: transformers have become the foundation of modern NLP models like BERT and GPT.

positive_passage: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) is a pre-trained language model developed by Google. It reads text bidirectionally and is used for tasks like question answering

GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) is a language model developed by OpenAI that generates human-like text. It is trained on large corpora using unsupervised learning and fine-tuned for various downstream tasks.
A vector database is a type of database that stores and retrieves data as high-dimensional vectors. It is optimized for similarity search and is used in applications like semantic search

FAISS (Facebook AI Similarity Search) is an open-source library developed by Facebook Research for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors. It supports both exact and approximate nearest neighbor search at scale.
Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is an AI technique that combines information retrieval with text generation. Instead of relying solely on parametric knowledge
column3: RAG retrieves relevant documents from an external knowledge base and uses them as context for generating accurate answers.

positive_passage: Cosine similarity is a metric used to measure the similarity between two non-zero vectors in a multi-dimensional space. It is calculated as the cosine of the angle between the vectors and ranges from -1 to 1

query: What is fine-tuning in machine learning?
positive_passage: Fine-tuning is the process of taking a pre-trained model and continuing to train it on a specific dataset for a particular task. It allows models to adapt their learned representations to a new domain while retaining general knowledge from pre-training.
An embedding is a dense vector representation of data such as words
column4: or images in a continuous vector space. Embeddings capture semantic relationships so that similar concepts have vectors that are close together in the embedding space.

positive_passage: The attention mechanism is a technique in neural networks that allows the model to focus on relevant parts of the input when producing an output. In transformers
column3: self-attention computes relationships between all pairs of positions in a sequence simultaneously.

positive_passage: BM25 (Best Match 25) is a probabilistic ranking function used in information retrieval. It ranks documents based on the query terms appearing in each document and considers term frequency and inverse document frequency to score relevance.
In the context of large language models
column3: hallucination refers to the generation of factually incorrect or nonsensical information that sounds plausible. It occurs when a model generates content not supported by its training data or provided context.

query: What is a large language model?
positive_passage: A large language model (LLM) is a type of AI model trained on massive amounts of text data using self-supervised learning. LLMs learn statistical patterns in language and can generate coherent text
column5: and perform many other language tasks.

positive_passage: Semantic search is a search technique that understands the intent and contextual meaning of a query rather than just matching keywords. It uses vector embeddings to find results that are semantically similar to the query even if exact words are not present.
A cross-encoder is a model that takes a pair of texts as input and produces a relevance score. In information retrieval
column3: cross-encoder reranking re-scores a shortlist of candidate passages retrieved by a faster bi-encoder

column4: and image embeddings. It provides pre-trained models that can encode sentences into fixed-size dense vectors useful for semantic search
